speaker: WARWICK
text: How now, my Lord Chief Justice, whither away?

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: How doth the King?

speaker: WARWICK
text: Exceeding well. His cares are now all ended.

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: I hope, not dead.

speaker: WARWICK
text: He’s walked the way of nature,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And to our purposes he lives no more.

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: I would his Majesty had called me with him.

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: The service that I truly did his life

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Hath left me open to all injuries.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Indeed, I think the young king loves you not.

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: I know he doth not, and do arm myself

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: To welcome the condition of the time,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Which cannot look more hideously upon me

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Than I have drawn it in my fantasy.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Here come the heavy issue of dead Harry.

speaker: WARWICK
text: O, that the living Harry had the temper

speaker: WARWICK
text: Of he the worst of these three gentlemen!

speaker: WARWICK
text: How many nobles then should hold their places

speaker: WARWICK
text: That must strike sail to spirits of vile sort!

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: O God, I fear all will be overturned.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Good morrow, cousin Warwick, good morrow.

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
              ,
               
              THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: Good morrow, cousin.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: We meet like men that had forgot to speak.

speaker: WARWICK
text: We do remember, but our argument

speaker: WARWICK
text: Is all too heavy to admit much talk.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Well, peace be with him that hath made us heavy.

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Peace be with us, lest we be heavier.

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
text: O, good my lord, you have lost a friend indeed,

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
text: And I dare swear you borrow not that face

speaker:
HUMPHREY
               
              OF
               
              GLOUCESTER
text: Of seeming sorrow; it is sure your own.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: Though no man be assured what grace to find,

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: You stand in coldest expectation.

speaker:
JOHN
               
              OF
               
              LANCASTER
text: I am the sorrier; would ’twere otherwise.

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: Well, you must now speak Sir John Falstaff fair,

speaker:
THOMAS
               
              OF
               
              CLARENCE
text: Which swims against your stream of quality.

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Sweet princes, what I did I did in honor,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Led by th’ impartial conduct of my soul;

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: And never shall you see that I will beg

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: A ragged and forestalled remission.

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: If truth and upright innocency fail me,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: I’ll to the king my master that is dead

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: And tell him who hath sent me after him.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Here comes the Prince.

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Good morrow, and God save your Majesty.

speaker: PRINCE
text: This new and gorgeous garment majesty

speaker: PRINCE
text: Sits not so easy on me as you think.—

speaker: PRINCE
text: Brothers, you mix your sadness with some fear.

speaker: PRINCE
text: This is the English, not the Turkish court;

speaker: PRINCE
text: Not Amurath an Amurath succeeds,

speaker: PRINCE
text: But Harry Harry. Yet be sad, good brothers,

speaker: PRINCE
text: For, by my faith, it very well becomes you.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Sorrow so royally in you appears

speaker: PRINCE
text: That I will deeply put the fashion on

speaker: PRINCE
text: And wear it in my heart. Why then, be sad.

speaker: PRINCE
text: But entertain no more of it, good brothers,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Than a joint burden laid upon us all.

speaker: PRINCE
text: For me, by heaven, I bid you be assured,

speaker: PRINCE
text: I’ll be your father and your brother too.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Let me but bear your love, I’ll bear your cares.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Yet weep that Harry’s dead, and so will I,

speaker: PRINCE
text: But Harry lives that shall convert those tears

speaker: PRINCE
text: By number into hours of happiness.

speaker: BROTHERS
text: We hope no otherwise from your Majesty.

speaker: PRINCE
text:
You all look strangely on me.
                 
                To
                 
                the
                 
                Chief
                 
                Justice
                .
              And you most.

speaker: PRINCE
text: You are, I think, assured I love you not.

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: I am assured, if I be measured rightly,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Your Majesty hath no just cause to hate me.

speaker: PRINCE
text: No? How might a prince of my great hopes forget

speaker: PRINCE
text: So great indignities you laid upon me?

speaker: PRINCE
text: What, rate, rebuke, and roughly send to prison

speaker: PRINCE
text: Th’ immediate heir of England? Was this easy?

speaker: PRINCE
text: May this be washed in Lethe and forgotten?

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: I then did use the person of your father;

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: The image of his power lay then in me.

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: And in th’ administration of his law,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Whiles I was busy for the commonwealth,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Your Highness pleasèd to forget my place,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: The majesty and power of law and justice,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: The image of the King whom I presented,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: And struck me in my very seat of judgment,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Whereon, as an offender to your father,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: I gave bold way to my authority

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: And did commit you. If the deed were ill,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Be you contented, wearing now the garland,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: To have a son set your decrees at nought?

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: To pluck down justice from your awful bench?

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: To trip the course of law and blunt the sword

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: That guards the peace and safety of your person?

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Nay more, to spurn at your most royal image

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: And mock your workings in a second body?

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Question your royal thoughts, make the case yours;

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Be now the father and propose a son,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Hear your own dignity so much profaned,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: See your most dreadful laws so loosely slighted,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: Behold yourself so by a son disdained,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: And then imagine me taking your part

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: And in your power soft silencing your son.

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: After this cold considerance, sentence me,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: And, as you are a king, speak in your state

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: What I have done that misbecame my place,

speaker:
CHIEF
               
              JUSTICE
text: My person, or my liege’s sovereignty.

speaker: PRINCE
text: You are right, justice, and you weigh this well.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Therefore still bear the balance and the sword.

speaker: PRINCE
text: And I do wish your honors may increase

speaker: PRINCE
text: Till you do live to see a son of mine

speaker: PRINCE
text: Offend you and obey you as I did.

speaker: PRINCE
text: So shall I live to speak my father’s words:

speaker: PRINCE
text: “Happy am I that have a man so bold

speaker: PRINCE
text: That dares do justice on my proper son;

speaker: PRINCE
text: And not less happy, having such a son

speaker: PRINCE
text: That would deliver up his greatness so

speaker: PRINCE
text: Into the hands of justice.” You did commit me,

speaker: PRINCE
text: For which I do commit into your hand

speaker: PRINCE
text: Th’ unstainèd sword that you have used to bear,

speaker: PRINCE
text: With this remembrance: that you use the same

speaker: PRINCE
text: With the like bold, just, and impartial spirit

speaker: PRINCE
text: As you have done ’gainst me. There is my hand.

speaker: PRINCE
text: You shall be as a father to my youth,

speaker: PRINCE
text: My voice shall sound as you do prompt mine ear,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And I will stoop and humble my intents

speaker: PRINCE
text: To your well-practiced wise directions.—

speaker: PRINCE
text: And, princes all, believe me, I beseech you:

speaker: PRINCE
text: My father is gone wild into his grave,

speaker: PRINCE
text: For in his tomb lie my affections,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And with his spirits sadly I survive

speaker: PRINCE
text: To mock the expectation of the world,

speaker: PRINCE
text: To frustrate prophecies, and to raze out

speaker: PRINCE
text: Rotten opinion, who hath writ me down

speaker: PRINCE
text: After my seeming. The tide of blood in me

speaker: PRINCE
text: Hath proudly flowed in vanity till now.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Now doth it turn and ebb back to the sea,

speaker: PRINCE
text: Where it shall mingle with the state of floods

speaker: PRINCE
text: And flow henceforth in formal majesty.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Now call we our high court of parliament,

speaker: PRINCE
text: And let us choose such limbs of noble counsel

speaker: PRINCE
text: That the great body of our state may go

speaker: PRINCE
text: In equal rank with the best-governed nation;

speaker: PRINCE
text: That war, or peace, or both at once, may be

speaker: PRINCE
text: As things acquainted and familiar to us,

speaker: PRINCE
text:
To
                 
                the
                 
                Chief
                 
                Justice
                .
                 
              In which you, father, shall have foremost hand.

speaker: PRINCE
text: Our coronation done, we will accite,

speaker: PRINCE
text: As I before remembered, all our state.

speaker: PRINCE
text: And, God consigning to my good intents,

speaker: PRINCE
text: No prince nor peer shall have just cause to say

speaker: PRINCE
text: God shorten Harry’s happy life one day.
